seo.title: PII Filtering for AI Agents in Amazon Bedrock Agents Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix pii filtering for ai agents in Amazon Bedrock Agents agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Amazon Bedrock Agents, PII Filtering failures occur when agents process sensitive data from knowledge bases, action groups, or user inputs without proper redaction, leading to leaks in responses or Lambda outputs. Common manifestations include unfiltered PII in knowledge base retrievals, action group Lambda functions returning raw sensitive data, and agent guardrails failing to catch domain-specific PII patterns. These issues are exacerbated by Bedrock's stateless session handling and lack of built-in PII detection for custom enterprise data.

howItManifests.scenario: Knowledge base retrieval returns customer records with unredacted SSNs
howItManifests.symptom: Agent responses contain full SSNs like '123-45-6789' from retrieved documents
howItManifests.impact: Compliance violations (GDPR, HIPAA) and data exposure to end users

howItManifests.scenario: Action group Lambda returns credit card numbers from database queries
howItManifests.symptom: Lambda malformed responses include raw PANs like '4111-1111-1111-1111'
howItManifests.impact: Action group execution fails validation and exposes PII in agent trace

howItManifests.scenario: Guardrails miss domain-specific PII like internal employee IDs
howItManifests.symptom: Agent outputs employee numbers like 'EMP-12345' without redaction
howItManifests.impact: Internal data leakage and failed enterprise security audits

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-turn conversations accumulate unfiltered PII in session state
howItManifests.symptom: Session summaries contain cumulative PII from prior turns
howItManifests.impact: Persistent storage of sensitive data across conversations

detection.name: Monitor invoke_agent() Responses for PII Patterns
detection.description: Intercepts Bedrock Agent responses and scans for common PII regex patterns before returning to user
detection.code:
import re
import boto3

bedrock_agent = boto3.client('bedrock-agent-runtime')

PII_PATTERNS = [
    r'\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b',  # SSN
    r'\b\d{4}-?\d{4}-?\d{4}-?\d{4}\b',  # Credit Card
    r'\b[A-Z]\d{6}\b',  # Employee ID
    r'\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b'  # SSN variant
]

def detect_pii_in_response(response_text):
    for pattern in PII_PATTERNS:
        if re.search(pattern, response_text):
            print(f'🚨 PII DETECTED: {pattern}')
            return True
    return False

response = bedrock_agent.invoke_agent(
    agentId='YOUR_AGENT_ID',
    agentAliasId='YOUR_ALIAS_ID',
    sessionId='session-123',
    inputText='Find customer John Doe'
)

if detect_pii_in_response(response['completion']):
    raise ValueError('PII detected in agent response')

detection.name: Audit Action Group Lambda Responses
detection.description: Validates Lambda function outputs from action groups contain no PII before agent processing

fixes.name: Pre-process Knowledge Base Documents
fixes.description: Redacts PII from documents before indexing in Bedrock Knowledge Base
fixes.code:
import boto3
import re

bedrock_agent = boto3.client('bedrock-agent')

PII_REPLACEMENT = {
    r'\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b': '[SSN]',
    r'\b\d{4}-?\d{4}-?\d{4}-?\d{4}\b': '[CARD]',
    r'\b[A-Z]\d{6}\b': '[EMPLOYEE_ID]'
}

def redact_pii(text):
    for pattern, replacement in PII_REPLACEMENT.items():
        text = re.sub(pattern, replacement, text)
    return text

# Pre-process documents before KB ingestion
original_doc = "John Doe (SSN: 123-45-6789) works at EMP123456"
redacted_doc = redact_pii(original_doc)
print(f"Redacted: {redacted_doc}")

# Upload to S3 for KB
s3 = boto3.client('s3')
s3.put_object(Bucket='my-kb-bucket', Key='docs/redacted.json', Body=redacted_doc)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating PII Filtering for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides continuous, runtime verification of Amazon Bedrock Agents by wrapping invoke_agent() calls with vex.verify(). This automatically detects PII leakage across knowledge base retrievals, action group responses, and final agent outputs using configurable regex patterns and ML-based PII classifiers. Vex maintains session-level PII tracking across multi-turn conversations and provides remediation templates for Lambda functions and guardrail configurations.

The platform integrates seamlessly with existing Bedrock deployments, requiring only a single line change to add comprehensive PII monitoring. Vex automatically generates remediation code for detected issues and provides compliance reports for audit trails. It handles Bedrock's streaming responses and action group complexity without requiring changes to agent configuration.
vexAutomation.code:
import vex

from vex.policies import Policy
from vex.integrations.bedrock import BedrockAgentVerifier

# Define PII policy
pii_policy = Policy(
    name="bedrock-pii-filtering",
    description="Detect PII in Bedrock Agent responses",
    patterns=[
        r'\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b',
        r'\b\d{4}-?\d{4}-?\d{4}-?\d{4}\b',
        r'\b[A-Z]\d{6}\b'
    ],
    action="block"
)

# Wrap Bedrock Agent calls
@verifier.verify(pii_policy)
def safe_invoke_agent(agent_id, session_id, input_text):
    response = bedrock_agent_runtime.invoke_agent(
        agentId=agent_id,
        agentAliasId='TSTALIASID',
        sessionId=session_id,
        inputText=input_text
    )
    return response

# Usage
result = safe_invoke_agent(
    'YOUR_AGENT_ID', 
    'session-123', 
    'Find customer records'
)

# Vex automatically blocks PII and logs violations
